features.summary: CoreSite delivered strong Q2 2021 results with operating revenues of $162 million (7.7% YoY growth) and FFO per share as adjusted of $1.42 (5.2% YoY growth). The company signed $7.8 million in new and expansion leases, with retail and small-scale leasing above trailing 12-month averages. Portfolio occupancy increased 220 basis points year-to-date to 84.1%. Management raised 2021 FFO guidance to $5.52-$5.60 per share and increased cash rent mark-to-market guidance to 2-4%.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued momentum in retail and small-scale leasing driven by hybrid multi-cloud demand, with larger-scale opportunities anticipated in the second half of 2021. The company is targeting portfolio occupancy in the high 80s range and expects to achieve mid-to-high single-digit growth in revenues, earnings, and FFO per share. Development projects including LA3 Phase 2 and NY2 expansion remain on track, with SV9 receiving zoning approval from Santa Clara City Council.
features.risks: Key risks include potential construction cost inflation impacting future development projects like SV9, though current projects are already contracted. The company has 7% revenue exposure to Chinese-domiciled customers amid ongoing US-China regulatory tensions. Power constraints in Silicon Valley could potentially delay SV9 development, though management indicates discussions with utilities are progressing well.
features.key_drivers: Growth is being driven by strong demand for edge computing and hybrid multi-cloud architectures, particularly in financial services and enterprise segments. The company's network-dense campus ecosystems and connectivity partnerships (including new Google Cloud availability) are attracting high-quality customers. Lease renewal spreads of 4.2% cash and 7.1% GAAP exceeded expectations, supported by the value proposition of CoreSite's interconnected platform and sticky customer deployments.
